query: Calcutta High Court Ritabrata Banerjee Leader of Opposition West Bengal June 2026 stay order hearing
answer: The research is complete. The key finding is that the **Calcutta High Court refused to grant a stay order** on the Speaker's decision recognizing Ritabrata Banerjee as Leader of Opposition in the West Bengal Assembly. The court declined a stay on both June 11 and June 16, 2026, meaning Ritabrata continues to function as LoP ahead of the Assembly session beginning June 18. The case remains ongoing with significant constitutional questions being examined, but no restraining order has been issued against the Speaker's decision.

sources.title: Calcutta High Court Set To Decide Legality Of Speaker's Decision Recognising Ritabrata Banerjee As Opposition Leader
sources.snippet: Calcutta High Court set to decide legality of Speaker's decision recognising Ritabrata Banerjee as Opposition Leader in West Bengal Assembly

key_facts: Following the 2026 West Bengal Assembly elections (April 23 & 29, counting May 4), BJP won ~158 seats, ending TMC's 15-year rule; TMC won ~130 of 294 seats.
Approximately 58 of TMC's ~80 MLAs rebelled against Mamata Banerjee's leadership after the election loss.
Around June 3, 2026, rebel TMC MLAs submitted signatures to Assembly Speaker Rathindra Bose backing Ritabrata Banerjee (an MLA whom TMC claims it expelled) as their legislative party leader.
Speaker Rathindra Bose recognized Ritabrata Banerjee as Leader of Opposition (LoP), ruling the expulsions were not valid under party constitution.
Official TMC faction nominated Sobhandeb Chattopadhyay as their LoP choice.
Around June 8, 2026, official TMC moved Calcutta High Court challenging the Speaker's decision.
On June 11, 2026, Justice Krishna Rao (single-judge vacation bench) REFUSED to grant any interim stay on the Speaker's decision recognizing Ritabrata as LoP.
On June 16, 2026, the court raised critical constitutional questions about whether the Speaker can suo moto ignore the majority party's LoP proposal, but again granted no stay.
TMC argued the Speaker's decision 'hits the Constitution's basic structure' and that an expelled MLA cannot be recognized as LoP without party consent.
Ritabrata Banerjee continues to function as Leader of Opposition as of June 17, 2026, with no stay order in place.
The West Bengal Assembly session was scheduled to begin June 18, 2026, meaning Ritabrata would function as LoP in the Assembly.
Parallel crisis at national level: 19-20 rebel TMC Lok Sabha MPs sought recognition as separate group from Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla.
